docs.title: ColorProperties
docs.summary: All Framework7-React components supports same set of color properties that allow to set separate element colors and color themes:
docs.retrieval_text:
All Framework7-React components supports same set of color properties that allow to set separate element colors and color themes:
Props: color, colorTheme, textColor, bgColor, borderTheme, rippleColor, dark
Events:

props.name: color
props.description: Single element color. One of the default colors.

props.name: colorTheme
props.description: Applies color theme to the element. It should be some parent element as this will have visual effect on all supported children elements, e.g. view, page, navbar, toolbar, list, etc. One of the default colors.

props.name: textColor
props.description: Sets element's text color. One of the default colors.

props.name: bgColor
props.description: Sets element's background color. One of the default colors.

props.name: borderTheme
props.description: Sets element's borders color. One of the default colors.

props.name: rippleColor
props.description: Sets element ripple wave color. One of the default colors.

props.name: dark
props.description: Enables dark layout theme on element. It should be some parent element as this will have visual effect on all supported children elements, e.g. view, page, navbar, toolbar, list, etc.

docs.content: All Framework7-React components supports same set of color properties that allow to set separate element colors and color themes:
